content.problemStatement:
A sequence XXX is defined as:


X1=0;X_1 = 0;X1​=0;
X2=A;X_2 = A;X2​=A;
X3=B;X_3 = B;X3​=B;
XiX_iXi​ = Xi−1+Xi−2−Xi−3X_{i-1} + X_{i-2} - X_{i-3}Xi−1​+Xi−2​−Xi−3​ for all i>3i > 3i>3.
Given an integer KKK, find the KthK^{th}Kth **smallest** element of sequence XXX.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three space-separated integers AAA, BBB, and KKK, as mentioned in the statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the KthK^{th}Kth smallest element in the sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The sequence is 0,18,13,31,…0, 18, 13, 31, \ldots0,18,13,31,… where the second smallest element is 131313.
Test case 222: The sequence is 0,50,30,80,60,110,90,140,120,…0, 50, 30, 80, 60, 110, 90, 140, 120, \ldots0,50,30,80,60,110,90,140,120,… where the seventh smallest element is 110110110.
